violations.text: Sushi was observed cooling in the sushi display case (open-air cooler). An open air cooler is not effective cooling equipment. Establishment must cool food in the blast chiller or freezer. Sushi was observed held at 44-54 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. All sushi was prepared less than 4 hours prior to inspection. Sushi was relocated to the freezer and rapidly cooled to less than 41 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

Reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) seafood (shrimp and tuna) were observed thawing with the package intact. ROP seafood must be completely removed from the package when thawing. ROP seafood was observed held at 26-36 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. ROP seafood was removed from the package during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025
